mainEntity.name: How much money will I actually get every week while I\'m out of work with a warehouse injury?

acceptedAnswer.text: Typically, you will receive about two-thirds of your average weekly wage, tax-free, up to a state maximum. This calculation should include your overtime and bonuses from the previous year.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, you typically receive 66 and 2/3% of your Average Weekly Wage (AWW), up to a state-mandated cap. These are called Temporary Total Disability (TTD) benefits. You don\'t get your "full" paycheck, but these payments are usually tax-free. In Idaho and Colorado, the percentages and caps change slightly every year, but the two-thirds rule is a common standard.

You only start getting paid once you have missed a certain number of days (usually 3 to 7 days, depending on the state).

Wage replacement factors:
- Your gross income (including overtime and bonuses) for the year before the injury.
- The "waiting period" your state requires before payments start.
- Whether your doctor has put you on "off-work" status or "light duty."

Review your TTD checks carefully. Insurance companies often "forget" to include your overtime when calculating your average wage, which can cost you hundreds of dollars.

A lawyer will perform an audit of your wages to ensure you are being paid the maximum rate allowed by state law.
